title: 10 best herbal essential oils for itchy skin
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for itchy skin are natural remedies extracted from plants, providing relief and soothing irritated skin. These oils help to reduce inflammation, itching, and discomfort associated with skin conditions. The best herbal essential oils for itchy skin include Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Urtica dioica, Eucalyptus globulus, Lavandula angustifolia, Hypericum perforatum, Saponaria officinalis, Chamomilla recutita, and Echinacea purpurea. If left untreated, itchy skin can lead to sleep disturbances, anxiety, and social isolation, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties. When applied topically, the oil's active compounds work to soothe irritated skin, reducing redness and itching caused by dryness, bug bites, or allergic reactions. The calming and relaxing effects of English lavender also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for itchiness, promoting a sense of tranquility and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: English Lavender essential oil helps reduce inflammation, which is often a underlying cause of itching skin, by decreasing swelling and redness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in English Lavender essential oil help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itching and irritation, promoting overall skin health.

Anxiolytic (calming) properties: English Lavender essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to itchiness, making it an effective natural remedy for skin irritations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Buds: Contain high concentrations of camphor and linalool, which provide relief from itching and discomfort caused by skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny morning, taking care not to bruise or damage the petals.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days, ensuring even air circulation and minimal exposure to direct sunlight.
Transfer the dried flowers to a clean glass jar and cover with 150ml of high-proof industrial ethanol at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the mixture daily.
Filter the infusion through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids and setting the filtered liquid aside for future use.

Distill the filtered liquid using a water bath or steam distillation apparatus for 2 hours at 100°c, collecting the resulting essential oil in a separate container and storing it in an airtight glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil contains flavonoids, which have potent antioxidant effects that soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate itchiness. When applied topically, pot marigold essential oil calms itchy sensations, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy, calm skin. Its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds work together to provide rapid relief from itchy skin issues.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Calendula essential oil has antibacterial properties, which help to prevent the growth and spread of bacteria that can cause itchiness and inflammation on the skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with itchy skin conditions, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing irritated skin.
Emollient and moisturizing properties: Calendula essential oil is rich in emollients and moisturizers that help to hydrate and soften the skin, reducing itchiness caused by dryness or irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, pot marigold roots help to soothe and calm itchy skin.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers at the peak of their blooming season in mid-summer.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours or air drying for 10 days.
Blend 100g of dried calendula flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil and let it infuse for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids after pressing.
Store the extracted essential oil in an amber-colored glass bottle in a cool dark place for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The oil is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness. It also has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce the production of histamine, a chemical released by the body during an allergic reaction. As a result, aloe essential oil provides instant relief from itching and discomfort, leaving skin feeling soft, smooth, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal aloe essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing itchy skin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in herbal aloe essential oil help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation, which may contribute to itchy skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.

Soothing and moisturizing: Herbal aloe essential oil has emollient and humectant properties that can help lock in moisture, soothe dryness and irritation, and provide long-lasting relief from itchy skin symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help soothe and calm itchy skin, making them an ideal choice for topical applications.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to relieve itchiness and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh aloe vera gel from mature plants during the early morning hours when it's most potent.
Combine the harvested aloe vera gel with 20ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a warm dark place for 24 to 48 hours allowing the ingredients to infuse and blend together harmoniously.
After the infusion process strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids completely.
Allow the essential oil to mature for an additional 3 to 7 days at room temperature before using it on the skin or storing it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties. It calms irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling, while also soothing itching caused by insect bites, minor burns, or skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis. Lemon balm's gentle, non-irritating formula makes it an effective natural remedy for sensitive skin, providing relief without harsh chemicals or artificial fragrances.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin, providing relief from discomfort.
Antibacterial property: The antibacterial properties of lemon balm essential oil help to prevent bacterial infections that can cause or exacerbate itchy skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.

Calming and soothing property: Lemon balm essential oil has a calming and soothing effect on the skin, which helps to reduce itching, irritation, and discomfort associated with itchy skin conditions like psoriasis and dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain citral, a natural antipruritic agent that helps relieve itching and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in early morning and rinse thoroughly with cold water.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 3-4 hours.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves using a clevenger apparatus at 100°c for 1 hour.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water by decanting and filter through coffee filters.
Store the extracted melissa officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight lids for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, tea tree oil can help reduce inflammation and soothe dry, irritated skin, providing instant relief from itching and discomfort. Its antimicrobial properties also combat fungal and bacterial infections that may be contributing to the itchiness, promoting a healthy and balanced skin environment. As a result, tea tree essential oils have been shown to effectively alleviate itchy skin symptoms, leaving skin feeling calm, clear, and comforted.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: TTO's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria, fungi, and yeast that can cause itching, inflammation, and infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: TTO's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as terpinen-4-ol, help to reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with skin conditions like eczema, acne, and psoriasis.
Antioxidant properties: TTO's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, leading to itching, dryness, and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tea tree roots contain bioactive compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them effective in reducing itchiness.

Leaves: The leaves of the tea tree plant are rich in aromatic compounds like eucalyptol and globulol, which provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help alleviate itchy skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a reliable source.
Wash the harvested leaves with cold distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the cleaned leaves in a still or apparatus at 100-150°f for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separator or decantation method.
Filter the extracted oil through a coffee filter paper or activated carbon to ensure purity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as eucalyptol and caryophyllene, work to reduce redness and swelling, while also combating bacterial and fungal infections that can cause itchiness. Additionally, the oil's moisturizing qualities help to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin, providing long-lasting relief from itchy discomfort. As a result, using Tasmanian blue gum essential oils can bring welcome relief to itchy skin sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin conditions, making it a potential natural remedy for itching caused by inflammation.

Antimicrobial: The essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may contribute to itchy skin, promoting a healthy skin environment and reducing the risk of infection.

Anti-pruritic: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil has been shown to have anti-itching (anti-pruritic) effects, which can provide relief from itching and discomfort associated with skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and other itchy skin disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant are effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy skin.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain antibacterial properties that help to combat bacterial infections that can cause itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning when they are most aromatic.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours or air-dry them naturally.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a copper still with 10-12% water and collect the essential oil after 4 hours.
Filter the collected oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the final eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It soothes dry, irritated skin and reduces redness by calming the skin's natural response to allergens or irritants. Additionally, its antimicrobial agents help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to itchiness. By balancing skin pH levels and promoting healthy skin function, wild geranium essential oils provide quick relief from itchy skin, leaving skin feeling calm, smooth, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and Anti-inflammatory: Wild Geranium oil helps to reduce inflammation and prevent infection, which can soothe and calm itchy skin caused by minor cuts, scrapes, or rashes.

Antimicrobial and Antiviral: The antimicrobial properties of Wild Geranium oil help to combat fungal and viral infections that may cause itchiness, such as eczema, ringworm, or athlete's foot, promoting healthy skin.

Emollient and Soothing: The essential oil has emollient properties that help to moisturize and soothe dry, itchy skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by conditions like psoriasis, dermatitis, or dry skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy skin.
Barks: Contain antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal and bacterial infections causing itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh geranium maculatum leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Cleanly rinse the plant material with distilled water to remove dirt and contaminants.
Steam distill the geranium maculatum leaves and flowers using 10% of plant material by weight for every kilogram of steam produced over 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil in a clean, glass container and filter it through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature protected from light and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as curcuminoids and terpenes, work together to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation. Additionally, curry plant essential oil has been shown to have analgesic properties, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. By applying the oil topically, individuals can experience rapid relief from itchy skin conditions, leaving their skin feeling smooth, calm, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties: Curry leaf essential oil has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in curry leaf essential oil help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin dryness, itchiness, and irritation, promoting healthier and more resilient skin.

Soothing and calming properties: Curry leaf essential oil has a calming effect on the skin, which can help reduce itching, scratching, and discomfort caused by itchy skin conditions such as eczema or psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the curry plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming itchy skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the curry plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and itching by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum leaves in the early morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Crush dried helichrysum italicum leaves into smaller pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their natural oils.
Steam distill the crushed leaves with 300ml of water for 30 minutes at a pressure of 0.5bar to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter paper to separate the essential oil from the plant material and any impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's natural antibacterial compounds also work to reduce redness and swelling, while its moisturizing properties hydrate the skin and relieve dryness. Additionally, sage oil's ability to balance the body's natural pH levels helps to regulate skin function and reduce itching caused by imbalances. Overall, sage essential oils provide a natural and effective solution for calming itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Antipruritic: Sage essential oil has antipruritic properties, which means it helps to reduce itching and scratching by reducing inflammation and relieving discomfort. This makes it effective in soothing itchiness caused by dry skin, bug bites, or skin allergies.

Antiseptic: The antiseptic properties of sage essential oil help to prevent infection and promote wound healing, which can be beneficial for itchy skin conditions like eczema, acne, or psoriasis that are prone to bacterial infections.

Anti-inflammatory: Sage essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with itchy skin conditions, making it a natural remedy for reducing the severity of itchiness caused by conditions like dermatitis, sunburns, or allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy skin and reducing redness and swelling.
Leaves: Sage leaves contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and itching, while also providing moisturizing benefits to soothe dry and irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly by rinsing them with distilled water and gently shaking off excess moisture.
Steam distill the cleaned leaves in a glass still using 10% of the leaf weight in water for 30 minutes.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the distillation residue using a separation funnel within 24 hours after distillation.
Transfer the extracted essential oil to dark-glass bottles and store them in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of thyme essential oil help to prevent infections that can exacerbate itchiness. When applied topically, thyme essential oil provides soothing relief, calming irritated skin and reducing the urge to scratch.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme essential oil's antibacterial properties help to eliminate bacteria that can cause or exacerbate itchy skin conditions, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in thyme essential oil reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations, making it a soothing remedy for itchy skin.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy skin conditions such as eczema or dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm itchy skin, reducing redness and discomfort.
Buds: Thyme buds are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess antiseptic and anti-irritant properties, making them effective in relieving itchiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems from your garden or a trusted supplier.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a stainless steel alembic still using 200ml of distilled water for 4 hours.
Filter the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a paper coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Mix the thymus vulgaris essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 3% essential oil to 97% carrier oil for topical use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy skin with herbal essential oils is to combine a few specific blends for maximum effectiveness. For dry, irritated skin, mix lavender oil with coconut oil and apply topically. For insect bites or mild dermatitis, try a blend of tea tree oil and lemon balm oil. Always dilute the essential oils in a carrier oil, as undiluted use can cause further irritation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy skin that are treated with herbal essential oils are eczema, acne, rosacea, and minor burns. Certain essential oils like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile oil have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching. They can also promote wound healing and reduce scarring when used topically.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, work to reduce redness and irritation, calming the skin and promoting a soothing effect. Additionally, Calendula's antiseptic properties help to prevent infections that can exacerbate itchiness, while its emollient properties moisturize and hydrate the skin, providing long-lasting relief from itchy skin conditions. This makes Calendula officinalis essential oil an effective natural remedy for itchy skin.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin, providing relief from discomfort.
Phenolic acids: They possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm itchy skin, reducing irritation and promoting healing.
Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help alleviate itchy skin by reducing inflammation, itching, and allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are used to make essential oils for itchy skin because they contain anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm irritated skin.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for itchy skin because they possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce redness and inflammation.
Roots: The roots are used to make essential oils for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect sensitive skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in full bloom when the petals are fully open and the stems are dry.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation to prevent mold formation.
Clean the flowers thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris that may affect the distillation process.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried flowers.
Collect the extracted essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's high concentration of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene and other nutrients, helps to calm and reduce redness in irritated skin. It also has antioxidant properties that can help to neutralize free radicals and promote skin health. Additionally, the oil's hydrating properties help to lock in moisture, providing relief from dry, itchy skin and promoting a smooth, healthy complexion.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloein helps with itchy skin by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated skin, thereby providing relief from itching and discomfort.
Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones exhibit anti-inflammatory and anti-itching properties, which help to calm and comfort itchy skin, reducing the urge to scratch.
Furanochromones: Furanochromones possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate itchy skin by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of irritated skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of essential oils in Aloe barbadensis, containing compounds like aloe-emodin and aloin, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Roots: Aloe roots also contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in reducing itchy skin and promoting wound healing.

Stems: The stems of Aloe barbadensis contain a compound called aloe saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them useful in treating itchy skin and other skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe barbadensis gel by cutting mature leaves and allowing them to dry for 24 to 48 hours.
Extract the gel from the dried aloe barbadensis leaves using a centrifuge or cheesecloth at 3000 rpm.
Filter the extracted gel through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any impurities and debris in 30 minutes.
Distill the filtered gel using steam distillation equipment at 100°c for 2 hours to produce essential oil.
Collect and store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compound, cineole, has been shown to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to combat underlying infections that may be causing the itchiness. By addressing the root cause of the issue, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil provides fast and effective relief from itchy skin, promoting a return to healthy, smooth skin.
remedy_constituents: Cineol: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and redness associated with itchy skin.
Limonene: Exhibits antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm itchy skin.
Methyl cinnamate: Demonstrates antipruritic effects, providing relief from itching and discomfort caused by skin irritations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high concentrations of the compound eucalyptol, which provides anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to soothe itchy skin.
Buds: Rich in eucalyptol and other volatile compounds, the buds help to calm and reduce inflammation in itchy skin.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia also contain eucalyptol and other beneficial compounds that aid in reducing itchiness and promoting skin health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The oil, derived from the plant also known as stinging nettle, has natural compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and redness. It also has a cooling effect that can provide immediate relief from itchy skin, making it an effective natural remedy for conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and hives. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Tricosanoides: These triterpenoid saponins have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce itching and irritation in the skin.

Alkaloids (e.g., stachydrine, caffeic acid, and ascorbic acid): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antihistaminic effects, which can help alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with skin irritations.

Caffeic acid esters (e.g., ursolic acid, oleanolic acid): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce itching, inflammation, and oxidative stress in the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Urtica dioica for essential oil production, as they contain high levels of anti-inflammatory and antihistamine compounds that help alleviate itchy skin.
Stems: The stems are also used to make essential oils due to their rich content of volatile compounds that provide relief from itching and inflammation.
Roots: The roots are utilized to produce essential oils that target itchy skin conditions, thanks to their concentration of anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 kg of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems from a well-maintained and pesticide-free area.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-120°c for 2-3 hours to release its essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separatory funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities or sediment within 15 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with itchy skin because its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties effectively soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's decongestant properties also help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus contains compounds that have a cooling effect on the skin, providing instant relief from itching and redness. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat various skin issues, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution to itchy skin.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It helps to reduce inflammation and itching by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent.
E-Phellandrene: It possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate itching and discomfort.
α-Pinene: It has an antipruritic effect, helping to relieve itching and inflammation by blocking the release of chemical mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high oil content and ability to provide a cooling and anti-inflammatory effect on itchy skin.
Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems are another source of essential oil, which contains compounds that can help to calm itchy skin and reduce redness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 kg of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature trees with high oil content.
Steam distill the harvested leaves for 3 hours using a 10:1 water to herb ratio.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decantation or centrifugation for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities for 20 minutes.
Store the final product in a dark glass container at 20°c to preserve its properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate provides a calming effect on irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Its antiseptic properties also help to prevent infection and promote healthy skin regeneration. Additionally, the oil's ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation can help to alleviate the emotional discomfort associated with itchy skin, making it a popular natural remedy for soothing itchy skin conditions.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce itching and inflammation associated with skin conditions.
Linalyl acetate: It possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm itchy skin, reducing discomfort and pain.
Caryophyllene oxide: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, providing relief from itchy skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe itchy skin.
Leaves: The leaves are also used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help calm itchy skin and reduce redness.
Stems: The stems contain a small amount of essential oil, which is often used in combination with the flowers and leaves to create a more comprehensive essential oil blend for itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 kg of fresh flowering stems of lavandula angustifolia at dawn when the dew is still present.
Steam distillation requires a still with a condenser to separate the essential oil from the water.
Distill the mixture for 2-3 hours at a temperature of 70-80 degrees celsius to extract the oil.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the water and filter it through a coffee filter paper.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that can cause itching. By promoting a healthy skin balance and fighting off unwanted microorganisms, Hypericum perforatum essential oils provide natural relief from itchy skin, promoting a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Naphthoquinone: Helps reduce inflammation and itching by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which soothe irritated skin.
Flavonoids: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help alleviate itching and redness associated with skin irritations.
Phenolic acids: Possesses potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help calm and protect the skin from further irritation, reducing itching sensations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties and flavonoids that aid in reducing inflammation and itching.
Roots: Rich in hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to help soothe itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at peak bloom for optimal oil quality.
Steam distill the fresh flowers in a 1:10 ratio with water at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and debris for clarity.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis essential oils helps with itchy skin because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's antimicrobial action also helps to reduce the presence of bacteria and other pathogens that can exacerbate itchiness. Additionally, Saponaria officinalis has a gentle astringent effect, which can help to reduce inflammation and redness, providing relief from itchy skin and promoting a more even skin tone. This natural remedy can be used to alleviate various types of itchiness.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, helping to soothe and calm itchy skin by reducing inflammation and itching sensations.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate itchy skin by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that may be contributing to the itch.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, helping to soothe and calm itchy skin by reducing inflammation and itching sensations, and promoting skin health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in saponins, which provide anti-inflammatory and soothing properties for itchy skin.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and glycosides that help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
Roots: High in saponins and other bioactive compounds that provide antiseptic and anti-inflammatory benefits for itchy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil contains apigenin and bisabolol, which have been shown to calm irritated skin and reduce redness. The antiseptic properties of the oil also help to prevent infections, which can exacerbate itchy skin conditions. Additionally, the oil's natural moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin, providing relief and comfort to those affected by itchy skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: Helps with itchy skin by reducing inflammation and providing a soothing effect on irritated skin.
Alpha-bisabolol oxide A: Assists in alleviating itchy skin by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antipruritic agent.
Furanobisabolol oxide B: Contributes to relieving itchy skin by possessing antipruritic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in calming skin irritation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used to make essential oils for itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: Used to make essential oils for itchy skin due to their soothing and calming effects.
Roots: Used to make essential oils for itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers of chamomilla recutita in the morning after the dew has evaporated and dry them using a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 8 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a distillation apparatus at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it using a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature to preserve its properties.
Label the essential oil bottle with the date of production and the name of the plant species to ensure accurate identification.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness. It also has antipruritic properties, which directly target and relieve itching sensations. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea essential oil has been shown to promote skin health and regeneration, making it a popular natural remedy for itchy skin conditions. Its benefits have been well-documented in traditional medicine.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: Helps to reduce inflammation and itching by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that contributes to allergic reactions.
Caffeic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate itchy skin by reducing redness and swelling.
Icariin: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which can help soothe itchy skin by reducing the production of inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high concentration of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high amount of caffeic acid and chicoric acid, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have shown to have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh flowers of echinacea purpurea in late summer when in full bloom with 30-40 flowers per pound required.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer using a dehydrator at 95f for 8-12 hours to prevent spoilage.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a 20-30 kilogram batch using a 6-8 hour distillation time and a 1:3 ratio of flowers to water.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separatory funnel and a collection time of 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Filter the essential oil using a 0.2 micron filter to remove any remaining impurities and achieve a 100% pure oil.
